title: Playbook authoring guide — pin the workload-default environment-mismatch lesson (12th rule)
description: Phase 3 vertex-ai re-smoke surfaced a lesson worth pinning in the playbook authoring guide: when a runtime workload defaults to an environment-specific value (project ID, cluster name, region), the default should match the deployed environment, not the developer's local sandbox. The travel runtime defaulted vertex_project via `{{ workload.gcp_project }}` which itself defaulted to `noetl-cluster` (local kind), so on GKE every Vertex AI call hit the wrong project until ops#60 flipped the default to `noetl-demo-19700101`. This was the actual root cause behind the AMBER → GREEN flip, NOT the GCP auth recipes I designed up front. The guide already captures 11 rules from the 9-round flagship arc; this round adds a 12th in a new 'Workload defaults' section between 'Keychain rules' and 'Step semantics'. Cap: 1 docs PR.

design_brief_for_codex.current_state: repos/docs/docs/reference/playbook_authoring_guide.md has 11 rules in 5 sections: Keychain rules (2), Step semantics (2), External HTTP calls (1), YAML and SQL quoting (2), GUI integration (4). Each rule has Good/Bad code blocks and an evidence pointer to a bridge/outbox result file.
design_brief_for_codex.target_state: 12th rule lives in a new 'Workload defaults' section inserted between 'Keychain rules' and 'Step semantics'. Rule title: 'Match environment-specific workload defaults to where the playbook runs'. Section heading is `## Workload defaults`. Rule heading is `### Match environment-specific workload defaults to where the playbook runs`.
design_brief_for_codex.rule_body: Explain the trap in two sentences: workload fields can chain via Jinja (`vertex_project: "{{ workload.gcp_project }}"`), and a default-of-a-default is invisible to every caller who doesn't override. When the playbook is registered in a deployed environment (GKE, production), defaults that mention concrete project/cluster/region names must match THAT environment, not the developer's local kind/sandbox.
design_brief_for_codex.good_block:
```yaml
workload:
  # GKE project — used by every downstream Jinja chain.
  gcp_project: "noetl-demo-19700101"
  vertex_project: "{{ workload.gcp_project }}"
  vertex_region: "us-central1"
```
design_brief_for_codex.bad_block:
```yaml
workload:
  # Local kind sandbox name — silently misroutes every GKE caller.
  gcp_project: "noetl-cluster"
  vertex_project: "{{ workload.gcp_project }}"
  vertex_region: "us-central1"
```
design_brief_for_codex.evidence_pointer: Surfaced in Phase 3 vertex-ai re-smoke. Evidence: `bridge/outbox/20260510-040000-travel-vertex-ai-resmoke.result.json` (and ops#60 commit message).
design_brief_for_codex.additional_paragraph_after_good_bad: After the Good/Bad blocks, add a one-paragraph note: 'When a playbook truly serves multiple environments, prefer making the field required (no default) and failing fast at register time, OR set the default to the production environment and require sandbox/local callers to override. The inverse — defaulting to local — silently breaks production. This pairs with the bare-keychain-references rule above: both expose how Jinja template chains in workload binding can hide a misconfiguration that only manifests at request time.'
design_brief_for_codex.no_other_changes: Do not modify any of the existing 11 rules, do not reorder sections beyond inserting the new one, do not touch the front-matter or the 'See also' footer. Single-section insertion only.

1_apply_edit.objective: Insert the new section between 'Keychain rules' (ending at the OAuth2 endpoint rule) and 'Step semantics' (starting at the `status: failed` rule).
spec: Locate the boundary between '## Keychain rules' (which ends with the 'Surfaced in round 2' evidence pointer for the OAuth2 keychain rule) and '## Step semantics'.

Insert the new `## Workload defaults` section with the rule heading, two-sentence body, Good code block, Bad code block, evidence pointer paragraph, and the additional one-paragraph note tying back to bare-keychain-references.
Verify: `cd repos/docs && npm install && npm run build` clean.
1_apply_edit.verify: Docs build clean; new section renders correctly in the resulting HTML.

spec: Commit using `git commit -F -` heredoc. Conventional-commits: `docs(reference): add 12th rule — match environment-specific workload defaults to where the playbook runs`. Body: cites Phase 3 vertex-ai re-smoke as the surfacing round; explains why the travel agent's default vertex_project flowed wrong on GKE; pairs with the bare-keychain-refs rule from round 2.
Push, PR, merge with `gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch`.
Commit: `chore(sync): bump docs for authoring-guide workload-defaults rule`.
3_bump_ai_meta_pointer.verify: ai-meta has a fresh local commit.

expected_state.description: playbook_authoring_guide.md has 12 rules across 6 sections; the new 'Workload defaults' section sits between 'Keychain rules' and 'Step semantics'.
expected_state.verify_shell: set -eo pipefail; cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta; HAS_SECTION=$(grep -c '^## Workload defaults' repos/docs/docs/reference/playbook_authoring_guide.md 2>/dev/null || echo 0); HAS_RULE=$(grep -c 'Match environment-specific workload defaults' repos/docs/docs/reference/playbook_authoring_guide.md 2>/dev/null || echo 0); HAS_EVIDENCE=$(grep -c '20260510-040000-travel-vertex-ai-resmoke' repos/docs/docs/reference/playbook_authoring_guide.md 2>/dev/null || echo 0); echo "section:$HAS_SECTION rule:$HAS_RULE evidence:$HAS_EVIDENCE"; if [ "$HAS_SECTION" -ge 1 ] && [ "$HAS_RULE" -ge 1 ] && [ "$HAS_EVIDENCE" -ge 1 ]; then echo 'GOAL: AUTHORING_GUIDE_WORKLOAD_DEFAULTS_LANDED'; else echo 'GOAL: NOT_YET'; fi

denied_tools: Modifying repos/noetl, repos/ops, repos/gui, repos/e2e — docs only round
Modifying any of the 11 existing rules in the guide
Touching the front-matter or 'See also' footer
preferred_tools: cd repos/docs && npm install && npm run build
git apply / git commit -F - / git push
gh pr create / gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch
result_file_instructions: Write bridge/outbox/20260510-181500-authoring-guide-workload-defaults.result.json. Required fields: {id, from: 'codex', to: 'claude-cowork', completed_at, overall_status, phases: {1_apply_edit: {section_inserted: bool, docs_build_clean: bool}, 2_open_and_merge_docs_pr: {pr_url, merged_sha}, 3_bump_ai_meta_pointer: {docs_sha, ai_meta_commit_local}}, regressions_found, iterations, verdict, notes}.
